utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan lists a health plan with base and addon costs totaling €400/month and includes features addressing medical services and stress management (mental health). This is fully specified with costs. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan provides EliteHome Premium Coverage, cost €95/month, with description; cost is specified, and it is home/contents protection—fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. The plan provides EliteTravel Premium Plan, €110/month, with description—fully specified. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan includes adult_children_transitional_coverage with estimated_cost €70/month and features—fully specified. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. It provides monthly breakdown including pre-discount, discount, discounted bundle, and final total; however, there is no annual breakdown. The requirement says monthly/annual cost breakdown; since only monthly is provided, it is partially specified and not fully complete. Therefore, 4 of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three named plans: PrimeCare Premium Plan (health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (property), and EliteTravel Premium Plan (travel). These exact plan names appear in the ground-truth ratings. The Wellness Programs Add-on corresponds to the 'Wellness Programs' item under health enhancement services. The adult_children_transitional_coverage is not a named plan from the ratings list, so it cannot be mapped. Therefore, map the three plans and the wellness add-on to their ratings.
